story.iWant: route-level authorization ile @Permission decorator ve PermissionsGuard
story.soThat: endpoint'leri granular permission check'ler ile koruyabileyim
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Create @Permission decorator (AC: 3.6.1) - Subtask 1.1: Create src/modules/permissions/decorators/permissions.decorator.ts - Subtask 1.2: Implement decorator with SetMetadata('permission', { module, action }) - Subtask 1.3: Export decorator from permissions module - Subtask 1.4: Update ActionEnum from permissions.constant.ts (if needed)
- Task 2: Create PermissionsGuard (AC: 3.6.2) - Subtask 2.1: Create src/modules/permissions/guards/permissions.guard.ts - Subtask 2.2: Implement CanActivate interface - Subtask 2.3: Inject Reflector and AuthorizationService via constructor - Subtask 2.4: Extract permission metadata from handler (Reflector.get('permission', context.getHandler())) - Subtask 2.5: Check @Public() decorator, if present → return true (skip check) - Subtask 2.6: Extract user from request.user (userID, domainID) - Subtask 2.7: Call AuthorizationService.hasPermission(userID, domainID, `${module}.${action}`) - Subtask 2.8: If hasPermission returns true → return true - Subtask 2.9: If hasPermission returns false → throw ForbiddenException with message including required permission - Subtask 2.10: Add guard to common module exports
- Task 3: Integrate guard with controllers (AC: 3.6.3) - Subtask 3.1: Add PermissionsGuard to common/guards/index.ts exports - Subtask 3.2: Example usage in UsersController: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard) + @Permission('USERS', ActionEnum.CREATE) on POST /users - Subtask 3.3: Verify guard order: JwtAuthGuard first (authenticate), then PermissionsGuard (authorize) - Subtask 3.4: Test @Public() decorator skip logic
- Task 4: Create unit tests for PermissionsGuard (AC: 3.6.2) - Subtask 4.1: Create src/modules/permissions/guards/permissions.guard.spec.ts - Subtask 4.2: Setup test module with mock Reflector, AuthorizationService, ExecutionContext - Subtask 4.3: Test: Permission granted (hasPermission true) → canActivate returns true - Subtask 4.4: Test: Permission denied (hasPermission false) → canActivate throws ForbiddenException - Subtask 4.5: Test: No @Permission metadata → return true (no permission required) - Subtask 4.6: Test: @Public() decorator present → return true (skip check) - Subtask 4.7: Test: Missing user in request → throw UnauthorizedException - Subtask 4.8: Run tests and verify coverage → ≥85% target
- Task 5: Integration testing (AC: 3.6.3) - Subtask 5.1: Create integration test for protected endpoint (e.g., POST /users) - Subtask 5.2: Test: User with USERS.CREATE permission → 201 Created - Subtask 5.3: Test: User without USERS.CREATE permission → 403 Forbidden - Subtask 5.4: Test: Missing JWT token → 401 Unauthorized (JwtAuthGuard) - Subtask 5.5: Test: Invalid JWT token → 401 Unauthorized - Subtask 5.6: Test: @Public() endpoint → accessible without auth - Subtask 5.7: Verify all existing controller tests still pass (no regressions)
- Task 6: Update documentation and examples - Subtask 6.1: Add decorator usage example to permissions module README - Subtask 6.2: Document guard setup in controller files (JSDoc comments) - Subtask 6.3: Update tech docs with guard flow diagram reference

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC-3.6.1: @Permission decorator oluşturulmuş - Decorator signature: @Permission(module: string, action: ActionEnum) - PermissionsGuard için metadata set ediyor - Example usage: @Permission('USERS', ActionEnum.CREATE) - Module ve action bilgileri reflector metadata olarak saklanıyor
AC-3.6.2: PermissionsGuard implemented - CanActivate interface extend ediyor - JwtAuthGuard'dan sonra execute oluyor (user zaten authenticated) - Reflector ile @Permission metadata'sını extract ediyor - AuthorizationService.hasPermission(userID, domainID, permission) çağırıyor - Permission granted → return true (request devam eder) - Permission denied → ForbiddenException throw (403 response) - @Public() decorator varsa check skip ediliyor
AC-3.6.3: Guard integration with controllers - @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard) ile controller'lara uygulanıyor - @Permission decorator ile birlikte çalışıyor - Example: Admin user create endpoint has @Permission('USERS', ActionEnum.CREATE) - Test: Permission olan user → 200/201 OK - Test: Permission olmayan user → 403 Forbidden - Test: @Public() decorator → permission check skip

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: User Management & RBAC Permissions
doc.section: Story 3.6: Permissions Guard & Decorator - Acceptance Criteria
doc.snippet: AC-3.6.1: @Permission decorator created with signature @Permission(module, action). AC-3.6.2: PermissionsGuard implements CanActivate, calls AuthorizationService.hasPermission(), throws ForbiddenException if denied. AC-3.6.3: Guard applied via @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard) with @Permission decorator on endpoints.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: User Management & RBAC Permissions
doc.section: Workflows and Sequencing - Permission Check Flow
doc.snippet: Permission Check Flow: Client → JwtAuthGuard (validate JWT) → PermissionsGuard (extract metadata, call AuthorizationService.hasPermission) → Controller (if authorized). ForbiddenException thrown if permission check returns false. Cross-domain access automatically blocked via domainID filtering.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: User Management & RBAC Permissions
doc.snippet: @Permission(module, action) decorator marks route permission requirement. PermissionsGuard executes permission checks. Execution order: JwtAuthGuard (authentication) → PermissionsGuard (authorization). @Public() decorator allows opt-out from checks.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: User Management & RBAC Permissions
doc.snippet: PermissionsGuard unit tests: Target ≥85% coverage. Mock Reflector, AuthorizationService, ExecutionContext. Test scenarios: Permission granted/denied, no metadata, @Public() present, missing user. Tools: Jest, @nestjs/testing.

doc.title: Story 3.5: Authorization Service
doc.snippet: AuthorizationService.hasPermission() fully implemented with signature hasPermission(userID, domainID, permission): Promise<boolean>. Optimized queries with eager loading, domainID filtering enforced. 13/13 unit tests passing with 100% coverage.

file.reason: Core method to call from PermissionsGuard - checks if user has specific permission with domainID filtering
Permission constants and ActionEnum type used in @Permission decorator signature
Reference for guard implementation pattern and @Public() decorator check. PermissionsGuard executes after this guard.
@Public() decorator constant (IS_PUBLIC_KEY) used to skip permission checks. PermissionsGuard must check for this metadata.
Reference test patterns for mocking PrismaService and testing service methods. Follow similar patterns for PermissionsGuard tests.
Module must export PermissionsGuard and @Permission decorator for use in controllers

story-context.constraints: - Guard must be stateless (no internal state between requests) - Performance target: <50ms execution time (calls optimized AuthorizationService) - Error messages must include required permission in ForbiddenException for debugging - @Public() decorator support is MANDATORY - must skip permission checks - Guard execution order is CRITICAL: JwtAuthGuard MUST execute before PermissionsGuard - Multi-tenancy: domainID must be extracted from request.user and passed to AuthorizationService - Permission format: Must combine module and action as 'MODULE.ACTION' string before calling hasPermission() - TypeScript strict mode: All code must compile with strict type checking - No database changes allowed - pure guard/decorator logic only

interface.name: AuthorizationService.hasPermission
interface.signature: async hasPermission(userID: string, domainID: string, permission: string): Promise<boolean>
interface.usage: Call from PermissionsGuard to check if authenticated user has required permission

interface.name: CanActivate interface
interface.usage: PermissionsGuard must implement this interface to integrate with NestJS guard system

interface.name: @Permission decorator signature
interface.usage: Decorator to mark routes with required permission. Sets metadata for PermissionsGuard to extract.

interface.name: Reflector.get / getAllAndOverride
interface.signature: get<T>(metadataKey: string, target: Function): T | undefined
interface.usage: Extract @Permission metadata and @Public() metadata from route handlers in PermissionsGuard

interface.name: ExecutionContext
interface.usage: Context parameter in canActivate() - use to extract handler metadata and request object

interface.name: request.user object
interface.signature: { userID: string, domainID: string, phoneNumber: string, roles: string[] }
interface.usage: Extract userID and domainID from authenticated user for permission check

tests.standards: Unit tests using Jest framework with @nestjs/testing utilities. Mock all dependencies (Reflector, AuthorizationService, ExecutionContext). Target: ≥85% coverage for PermissionsGuard. Test file location: src/modules/permissions/guards/permissions.guard.spec.ts. Follow patterns from authorization.service.spec.ts for mocking and test structure. Integration tests verify guard chain (JwtAuthGuard → PermissionsGuard) with real endpoints.
tests.locations: - src/modules/permissions/guards/permissions.guard.spec.ts (unit tests - NEW) - test/ directory for integration/e2e tests - Follow existing test patterns in src/modules/permissions/services/authorization.service.spec.ts
tests.ideas: - AC-3.6.1: Test @Permission decorator sets correct metadata (unit) - AC-3.6.2: Test PermissionsGuard.canActivate returns true when hasPermission returns true (unit) - AC-3.6.2: Test PermissionsGuard throws ForbiddenException when hasPermission returns false (unit) - AC-3.6.2: Test PermissionsGuard returns true when no @Permission metadata present (unit) - AC-3.6.2: Test PermissionsGuard returns true when @Public() decorator present (unit) - AC-3.6.2: Test PermissionsGuard throws UnauthorizedException when request.user missing (unit) - AC-3.6.2: Test PermissionsGuard extracts userID and domainID from request.user correctly (unit) - AC-3.6.2: Test PermissionsGuard combines module and action as 'MODULE.ACTION' string (unit) - AC-3.6.3: Integration test: POST /users with USERS.CREATE permission succeeds (201) - AC-3.6.3: Integration test: POST /users without USERS.CREATE permission fails (403) - AC-3.6.3: Integration test: Missing JWT token fails (401) - AC-3.6.3: Integration test: @Public() endpoint accessible without auth (200) - AC-3.6.3: Regression test: Verify all existing controller tests still pass
